---
title: "project01/README"
names:  Jacqueline Caldwell, Micheal Bambha
output: html_document
---

# Introduction
Project One:
Read in clinvar.vcf file; to identify AF_EXAC (allele frequencies) showing
rarity of the variant ; and create a list of associated clinical disease 
varients.

# Pseudocode
Put pseudocode in this box:

```
glob_count_clinical_diseases = 0
glob_clinical_diseases = ""

def parse_line(str1)
# Note string sent will have \n at the end.
  Look for "AF_EXAC" in str1 and get value afterwards (dictionary)
  Look for "CLND" and get value(s)? afterwards (dictionary)
     if value is < 0.0001
      if (ASSOCIATED_DISEASE != "") and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_specified') and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_provided')
        return ASSOCIATED_DISEASES string
      else
        return ""

def read_next_line(fp):
  # fp = file pointer
  # needs to return string with \n at end
     index = seek(fp,1) # read next character in file line
     while ( index != EOF ) and index is not '\n'
        line_string = line_string + single index character
        seek index forward one character
        
    # At this point first character should be the start of     
     If index == EOF 
          return ""   # I may be missing the last line.....
     elif: character is "\n"
          assoc_diseases = parse_string (line_string)
     else:
          add character to string
          seek to next character.
    append assoc_diseases to global_clinical_diseases
    global_clinical_diseases++
    return 

def read_file(file_name_str):
  file_pointer = open(file_name_str)
  
  Move file_pointer past file header before starting "read_line process"
      then...
      
  while file_pointer not at EOF:
      next_line = read_next_line(file_pointer) 
      
  close(file_pointer)
  
read_file("clinvar_20190923_short.vcf)
```

# Successes
Description of the team's learning points

# Struggles
Difficulty uploading files to GitHub; if working "on-demand"; must move files 
from server to my private machine, and then push them to github.  Tried working
on RStudio on my Mac, but it doesn't have KnitR ; tried colab but it uses 
different ways of setting current directories; but colab has a better debugging
situation.


# Personal Reflections
## Group Leader
Group leader's reflections on the project

## Other member
Other members' reflections on the project

# Generative AI Appendix
As per the syllabus
